1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Agreement” means this SaaS Subscription Agreement, including all referenced documents and Order Forms.  
1.2 “Customer” means the entity identified in the applicable Order Form subscribing to the Service.  
1.3 “Service” means the online software-as-a-service platform provided by Provider as described in the Order Form.  
1.4 “Order Form” means the ordering document executed by the parties specifying the Service, Fees, Subscription Term, and other relevant details.  
1.5 “Subscription Term” means the period during which Customer is authorized to access and use the Service, as specified in the Order Form.  
1.6 “Fees” means the subscription fees and any other charges payable by Customer for the Service, as set forth in the Order Form.  
1.7 “Customer Data” means all data, content, and materials uploaded to or processed by the Service by or on behalf of Customer.  
1.8 “Uptime Percentage” means the percentage of total minutes in a calendar month during which the Service is available, excluding scheduled maintenance and force majeure events.  
1.9 “SLA Credit” means a service credit issued to Customer in accordance with Section 4.  
1.10 “Provider” means the entity providing the Service, as identified in the Order Form.

2. SUBSCRIPTION SCOPE  
2.1 Subject to payment of Fees, Provider grants Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to access and use the Service during the Subscription Term solely for Customer’s internal business purposes, in accordance with this Agreement and the Order Form.  
2.2 Customer shall not (a) sublicense, sell, or resell the Service; (b) reverse engineer or attempt to derive the source code of the Service; or (c) use the Service to provide services to third parties.

3. FEES AND RENEWAL  
3.1 Customer shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of invoice date. Late payments are subject to a 1.5% monthly interest or the maximum allowed by law, whichever is less.  
3.2 Unless otherwise stated in the Order Form, the Subscription Term will automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current Subscription Term.  
3.3 Provider may increase Fees for any renewal term by providing at least ninety (90) days’ written notice prior to the renewal date.

4. UPTIME SLA AND CREDITS  
4.1 Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain an Uptime Percentage of at least 99.5% each calendar month.  
4.2 If the Uptime Percentage falls below 99.5% in any calendar month, Customer may request an SLA Credit equal to 5% of the monthly Fees for each 1% below the SLA, up to a maximum of 20% of the monthly Fees.  
4.3 To receive an SLA Credit, Customer must submit a written request within fifteen (15) days after the end of the affected month. SLA Credits are applied to future invoices and are Customer’s sole remedy for Service unavailability.

5. DATA OWNERSHIP  
5.1 As between the parties, Customer retains all rights, title, and interest in and to Customer Data.  
5.2 Provider will not access, use, or disclose Customer Data except as necessary to provide the Service, prevent or address technical problems, or as required by law.

6. SECURITY  
6.1 Provider will implement and maintain reasonable administrative, physical, and technical safeguards designed to protect the security, confidentiality, and integrity of Customer Data.  
6.2 In the event of a confirmed unauthorized access to Customer Data, Provider will notify Customer without undue delay and cooperate with Customer to remediate the incident.

7. SUSPENSION  
7.1 Provider may suspend Customer’s access to the Service upon written notice if (a) Customer fails to pay undisputed Fees within fifteen (15) days after written notice of late payment, or (b) Customer’s use of the Service poses a security risk or violates applicable law.  
7.2 Provider will promptly restore access once the issue is resolved.

8. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
8.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR BREACH OF SECTION 5 (DATA OWNERSHIP), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY.  
8.2 PROVIDER’S TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ALL CLAIMS ARISING UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CUSTOMER TO PROVIDER IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

9. TERM AND TERMINATION  
9.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for the Subscription Term, including any renewal terms, unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Section.  
9.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party if such breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.  
9.3 Upon termination, Customer’s right to access the Service ceases, and Provider will make Customer Data available for download for thirty (30) days after termination.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 5 (Data Ownership), 6 (Security), 8 (Limitation of Liability), 9.3 (Post-Termination Data Access), and this Section 10 survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.  
10.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the Order Form.  
10.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the Service and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement is effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
10.5 If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions remain in effect.  
10.6 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the other party’s prior written consent, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
10.7 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the jurisdiction specified in the Order Form, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- The agreement grants the Customer a limited, non-transferable right to use the Provider’s SaaS platform for internal business purposes during the specified subscription period, as long as all fees are paid.

- The Customer is prohibited from sublicensing, selling, reselling, reverse engineering, or using the Service to provide services to third parties.

- All fees must be paid within 30 days of the invoice date. Overdue payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower.

- Unless otherwise indicated in the Order Form, the subscription automatically renews for additional one-year terms unless either party gives written notice of non-renewal at least 60 days before the current term ends.

- The Provider may increase fees for renewal periods by giving at least 90 days’ written notice before the renewal date.

- The Provider aims to maintain at least 99.5% uptime each month. If uptime drops below this threshold, the Customer can request a service credit equal to 5% of the monthly fee for each 1% below the SLA, up to a maximum of 20% of the monthly fee. Requests for credits must be made in writing within 15 days after the affected month, and credits are applied to future invoices.

- The Customer retains ownership of all data uploaded or processed through the Service. The Provider will only access or use Customer Data as needed to deliver the Service, resolve technical issues, or comply with legal requirements.

- The Provider will maintain reasonable security measures to protect Customer Data. In the event of confirmed unauthorized access, the Provider will notify the Customer promptly and assist in resolving the issue.

- The Provider may suspend access to the Service if the Customer fails to pay undisputed fees within 15 days after receiving written notice, or if the Customer’s use creates a security risk or violates the law. Access will be restored once the issue is resolved.

- Except for cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct, or breaches of data ownership, neither party is liable for indirect or consequential damages. The Provider’s total liability is capped at the amount of fees paid by the Customer in the 12 months preceding the claim.

- The agreement starts on the Effective Date and continues for the subscription term and any renewals, unless terminated earlier. Either party can terminate for material breach if not remedied within 30 days of written notice.

- Upon termination, the Customer’s access to the Service ends. The Provider will make Customer Data available for download for 30 days after termination.

- Provisions regarding data ownership, security, limitation of liability, post-termination data access, and miscellaneous terms remain in effect after termination.

- All notices must be in writing and sent to the addresses listed in the Order Form.

- The agreement is the complete understanding between the parties and can only be changed in writing signed by both parties.

- If any part of the agreement is found invalid, the rest remains enforceable.

- Assignment of the agreement requires prior written consent from the other party, except in cases of assignment to an affiliate or as part of a merger or asset sale.

- The governing law is specified in the Order Form, excluding its conflict of law rules.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO SAAS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made effective as of the date last signed below. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the SaaS Subscription Agreement (the "Agreement") between the parties, the following terms are hereby amended:

1. Section 3.1 is amended to replace "thirty (30) days of invoice date" with "forty-five (45) days of invoice date." Customer shall pay all Fees within forty-five (45) days of invoice date.

2. Section 4.2 is amended to replace "up to a maximum of 20% of the monthly Fees" with "up to a maximum of 10% of the monthly Fees." If the Uptime Percentage falls below 99.5% in any calendar month, Customer may request an SLA Credit equal to 5% of the monthly Fees for each 1% below the SLA, up to a maximum of 10% of the monthly Fees.

All other terms and conditions of the Agreement remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

MANUFACTURING SUPPLY AGREEMENT

This Manufacturing Supply Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the following parties:

Supplier: [Supplier Name], with its principal place of business at [Supplier Address] (“Supplier”)

Customer: [Customer Name], with its principal place of business at [Customer Address] (“Customer”)

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Products” means the goods to be manufactured and supplied by Supplier to Customer as described in Exhibit A attached hereto.  
1.2 “Specifications” means the technical and quality requirements for the Products as set forth in Exhibit A.  
1.3 “Minimum Order Quantity” or “MOQ” means the minimum number of units per Product per order, as specified in Section 3.  
1.4 “Lead Time” means the period between Customer’s purchase order and delivery of Products, as specified in Section 4.  
1.5 “Price” means the purchase price per unit of Product, as set forth in Exhibit B, subject to adjustment as provided in Section 5.  
1.6 “Quality Acceptance Window” means the period after delivery during which Customer may inspect and reject non-conforming Products, as specified in Section 6.  
1.7 “Warranty Period” means the period during which Supplier warrants the Products, as specified in Section 7.  
1.8 “Recall” means any removal or correction of Products required by law or initiated by either party due to safety or regulatory concerns.  
1.9 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature below.

2. PRODUCTS AND SPECIFICATIONS  
2.1 Supplier shall manufacture and supply the Products in accordance with the Specifications.  
2.2 Any changes to the Specifications must be agreed in writing by both parties.

3. MINIMUM ORDER QUANTITY  
3.1 Customer shall order no less than 1,000 units per Product per purchase order.  
3.2 Supplier is not obligated to accept orders below the MOQ.

4. LEAD TIME  
4.1 Supplier shall deliver Products within 45 days of receipt of a valid purchase order.  
4.2 If Supplier anticipates a delay, Supplier shall notify Customer in writing within 5 business days of becoming aware of such delay.

5. PRICING AND ADJUSTMENT CAPS  
5.1 The Price for each Product is set forth in Exhibit B.  
5.2 Supplier may not increase the Price by more than 5% in any 12-month period.  
5.3 Supplier shall provide at least 60 days’ written notice of any proposed Price increase.  
5.4 Price decreases shall be passed on to Customer immediately upon occurrence.

6. QUALITY ACCEPTANCE WINDOW  
6.1 Customer shall inspect Products within 15 days of delivery (“Quality Acceptance Window”).  
6.2 Customer may reject Products that do not conform to the Specifications by providing written notice to Supplier within the Quality Acceptance Window.  
6.3 If Customer does not provide notice of rejection within the Quality Acceptance Window, Products shall be deemed accepted.

7. WARRANTY PERIOD  
7.1 Supplier warrants that Products will conform to the Specifications and be free from defects in material and workmanship for 24 months from the date of delivery (“Warranty Period”).  
7.2 During the Warranty Period, Supplier shall, at its own expense, repair or replace any defective Products within 30 days of written notice from Customer.

8. RECALL OBLIGATIONS  
8.1 If a Recall is required, Supplier shall bear all costs and expenses related to the Recall, including but not limited to transportation, replacement, and disposal of affected Products.  
8.2 Supplier shall promptly notify Customer of any potential Recall and cooperate fully with Customer in executing the Recall.

9. TERM AND TERMINATION  
9.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of three (3) years (“Initial Term”).  
9.2 Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms unless either party provides at least 90 days’ written notice of non-renewal prior to the end of the then-current term.  
9.3 Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party, provided that the breaching party fails to cure such breach within 30 days of written notice.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 6 (Quality Acceptance Window), 7 (Warranty Period), 8 (Recall Obligations), and this Section 10 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for the duration of their respective periods.  
10.2 Notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered to the addresses set forth above.  
10.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.4 No amendment or waiver of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
10.5 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of [Jurisdiction], without regard to its conflict of law principles.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

[Supplier Name] ___________________________ Date: ___________

[Customer Name] __________________________ Date: ___________
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Products: Supplier will produce and deliver goods as detailed in Exhibit A, adhering to the technical and quality standards specified therein. Any modifications to these standards require mutual written consent.

- Minimum Order Quantity: Each purchase order from Customer must be for at least 1,000 units per product. Supplier is not required to fulfill orders below this threshold.

- Lead Time: Supplier must ship ordered Products within 45 days after receiving a valid purchase order. If a delay is expected, Supplier must inform Customer in writing within 5 business days of becoming aware of the delay.

- Pricing: Unit prices are listed in Exhibit B. Supplier may not raise prices by more than 5% within any 12-month period and must give Customer at least 60 days’ advance written notice before any price increase. Any price reductions must be applied to Customer immediately.

- Quality Acceptance: Customer has 15 days from delivery to inspect Products and notify Supplier in writing of any non-conformance to specifications. If no rejection notice is given within this period, Products are considered accepted.

- Warranty: Supplier guarantees that Products will meet specifications and be free from material and workmanship defects for 24 months from delivery. During this period, Supplier must repair or replace defective Products at its own cost within 30 days of receiving written notice from Customer.

- Recall: If a recall is necessary, Supplier is responsible for all associated costs, including transport, replacement, and disposal. Supplier must promptly inform Customer of any potential recall and fully cooperate in the recall process.

- Term and Renewal: The Agreement starts on the Effective Date and lasts for an initial three-year term. It will automatically renew for additional one-year periods unless either party gives at least 90 days’ written notice before the current term ends to prevent renewal. Either party may terminate the Agreement for material breach if the breach is not remedied within 30 days after written notice.

- Survival: The provisions regarding quality acceptance, warranty, recall, and miscellaneous terms remain effective for their respective durations even after the Agreement ends.

- Notices: All formal communications must be in writing and sent to the parties’ addresses as stated in the Agreement.

- Governing Law: The Agreement is governed by the laws of the specified jurisdiction, excluding its conflict of law rules.

- Entire Agreement and Amendments: This document represents the complete agreement between the parties and overrides any previous agreements. Changes or waivers must be in writing and signed by both parties.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO MANUFACTURING SUPPLY AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made as of the Effective Date by and between Supplier and Customer as defined in the Agreement. The parties hereby agree to amend the Manufacturing Supply Agreement as follows:

1. Section 3.1 (Minimum Order Quantity) is hereby amended to read: "Customer shall order no less than 750 units per Product per purchase order."

2. Section 7.1 (Warranty Period) is hereby amended to read: "Supplier warrants that Products will conform to the Specifications and be free from defects in material and workmanship for 18 months from the date of delivery ('Warranty Period')."

All other terms and conditions of the Agreement remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Amendment as of the Effective Date.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Service Provider, as identified in the applicable Statement of Work (“SOW”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Affiliate” means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party.  
1.2 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.  
1.3 “Deliverables” means all work product, reports, materials, and other items to be delivered by Service Provider to Client under any SOW.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider as set forth in each SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed under this Agreement.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider as described in each SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to an SOW.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet any performance standards set forth in the SLA, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  
4.1 Except as otherwise set forth in an SOW, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables (excluding Service Provider’s pre-existing materials and third-party materials) shall be assigned to Client upon full payment of Fees.  
4.2 Service Provider grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free license to use Service Provider’s pre-existing materials incorporated in the Deliverables solely as part of the Deliverables.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY  
5.1 Each party shall protect the other party’s Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.  
5.2 Confidential Information may only be used for the purposes of performing this Agreement and may not be disclosed to third parties except to Affiliates, employees, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those herein.  
5.3 The obligations in this Section 5 shall survive for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement, except for trade secrets, which shall be protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

6. DATA PROTECTION  
6.1 Each party shall comply with all applicable data protection laws in connection with the processing of Personal Data under this Agreement.  
6.2 Service Provider shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction, or damage.  
6.3 If Service Provider becomes aware of a Personal Data breach, it shall notify Client without undue delay and cooperate with Client to address the breach.

7. WARRANTIES  
7.1 Service Provider warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and that Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable SOW for ninety (90) days after delivery.  
7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SERVICE PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.

8. INDEMNITY  
8.1 Service Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its Affiliates from and against any third-party claims, damages, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising from (a) Service Provider’s breach of this Agreement, or (b) allegations that the Deliverables infringe any third-party Intellectual Property Rights, except to the extent such claims arise from Client’s modifications or misuse.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
9.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM SECTION 8 (INDEMNITY), SECTION 5 (CONFIDENTIALITY), OR FOR BREACH OF PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.  
9.2 EACH PARTY’S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR $250,000, WHICHEVER IS LESS.

10. TERMINATION  
10.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for convenience upon sixty (60) days’ prior written notice.  
10.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately upon written notice if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice.  
10.3 Upon termination, Client shall pay Service Provider for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered up to the effective date of termination.

11. GOVERNING LAW  
11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
12.1 Sections 4 (Intellectual Property), 5 (Confidentiality), 6 (Data Protection), 8 (Indemnity), 9 (Limitation of Liability), 11 (Governing Law), and this Section 12 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.  
12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
12.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Services and Deliverables: The Service Provider is required to perform the agreed services and deliver specified work products as outlined in each Statement of Work (SOW) and any attached Service Level Agreement (SLA).

- Service Levels: The Service Provider must use commercially reasonable efforts to achieve at least 99.5% uptime each calendar month, as specified in the SLA.

- Payment Terms: The Client must pay all invoiced fees within 30 days of receiving a valid invoice. Overdue payments will accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower. If undisputed fees remain unpaid after the due date, the Service Provider may suspend services with 10 days’ written notice.

- Intellectual Property: Unless otherwise stated in an SOW, all intellectual property rights in the deliverables (excluding the Service Provider’s pre-existing or third-party materials) transfer to the Client once all fees are paid. The Client receives a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free license to use any of the Service Provider’s pre-existing materials included in the deliverables, but only as part of those deliverables.

- Confidentiality: Both parties must protect each other’s confidential information with at least reasonable care and may only use it to fulfill this agreement. Disclosure is limited to affiliates, employees, or contractors who need to know and are bound by similar confidentiality obligations. These confidentiality obligations last for three years after termination, except for trade secrets, which are protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

- Data Protection: Both parties must comply with applicable data protection laws regarding personal data processed under this agreement. The Service Provider must implement suitable technical and organizational safeguards against unauthorized access, loss, or damage to personal data. If a data breach occurs, the Service Provider must promptly notify the Client and cooperate in resolving the issue.

- Warranties: The Service Provider guarantees that services will be performed professionally and that deliverables will substantially meet the SOW specifications for 90 days after delivery. All other warranties are disclaimed unless expressly stated.

- Indemnification: The Service Provider will defend and indemnify the Client and its affiliates against third-party claims, damages, or expenses (including reasonable legal fees) resulting from the Service Provider’s breach of the agreement or claims that deliverables infringe third-party intellectual property rights, except where such claims result from the Client’s modifications or misuse.

- Limitation of Liability: Except for indemnity, confidentiality, or payment breaches, neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages. The maximum total liability for either party is capped at the lesser of the total fees paid or payable under the relevant SOW in the 12 months before the claim, or $250,000.

- Termination: Either party may terminate the agreement or any SOW for any reason with 60 days’ written notice. Immediate termination is allowed if the other party materially breaches the agreement and does not remedy the breach within 30 days of written notice. Upon termination, the Client must pay for all services performed and deliverables provided up to the termination date.

- Governing Law: The agreement is governed by the laws of the State of New York, excluding its conflict of law rules.

- Survival and Miscellaneous: Provisions regarding intellectual property, confidentiality, data protection, indemnity, limitation of liability, governing law, and miscellaneous terms remain effective after termination. Notices must be in writing and sent to the addresses listed in the SOW. The agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Amendments or waivers must be in writing and signed by both parties. Assignment is not permitted without prior written consent, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets. If any part of the agreement is found invalid, the rest remains enforceable.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made as of the date of last signature below and modifies the Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") as follows:

1. Section 2.2 is hereby amended to change the minimum uptime requirement from 99.5% per calendar month to 98.0% per calendar month.

2. Section 10.1 is hereby amended to change the notice period for termination for convenience from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days’ prior written notice.

All other terms and conditions of the Agreement remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

API AND DATA ACCESS AGREEMENT

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “API” means the application programming interface and related documentation provided by Provider to enable access to the Data.  
1.2 “Data” means all information, content, and materials made available via the API.  
1.3 “Provider” means the entity granting access to the API and Data under this Agreement.  
1.4 “Client” means the entity receiving access to the API and Data under this Agreement.  
1.5 “Permitted Users” means Client’s employees and contractors authorized to access the API and Data solely on Client’s behalf.  
1.6 “Fees” means the charges payable by Client for access to the API and Data, as set forth in Section 4.  
1.7 “Overage Charges” means additional fees incurred by Client for exceeding the Access Scope or Rate Limits.  
1.8 “Access Scope” means the permitted uses, endpoints, and data fields accessible to Client as specified in Section 2.  
1.9 “Rate Limits” means the maximum number of API calls permitted per minute, hour, or day, as specified in Section 2.  
1.10 “SLA” means the service level agreement for API uptime as set forth in Section 5.  
1.11 “Term” means the duration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 10.

2. ACCESS SCOPE AND RATE LIMITS  
2.1 Provider grants Client a non-exclusive, non-transferable right for Permitted Users to access and use the API and Data solely for Client’s internal business purposes.  
2.2 Access Scope: Client may access the following API endpoints: /users, /transactions, /reports. Data fields are limited to those described in the API documentation.  
2.3 Rate Limits: Client may make up to 10,000 API calls per day and no more than 500 calls per minute.  
2.4 Provider may monitor usage and notify Client in writing if Rate Limits are exceeded. Repeated or material breaches may result in suspension under Section 8.

3. PERMITTED USE  
3.1 Client shall not (a) sublicense, sell, or otherwise provide the API or Data to any third party; (b) use the API or Data to create a competing product or service; or (c) use the API or Data for any unlawful purpose.  
3.2 Client shall ensure that only Permitted Users access the API and Data and that all access credentials are kept secure.

4. FEES AND OVERAGE CHARGES  
4.1 Client shall pay Provider a monthly Fee of $2,000, payable within 30 days of invoice.  
4.2 If Client exceeds the Rate Limits, Overage Charges of $0.10 per additional API call will apply, invoiced monthly in arrears.  
4.3 Provider reserves the right to adjust Fees and Overage Charges upon 60 days’ written notice.

5. UPTIME SLA  
5.1 Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure the API is available 99.5% of each calendar month, excluding scheduled maintenance (with at least 48 hours’ notice) and force majeure events.  
5.2 If uptime falls below 99.5% in any month, Client may request a service credit equal to 5% of the monthly Fee for that month.  
5.3 Service credits are Client’s sole remedy for breach of the SLA.

6. DATA RETENTION PERIOD  
6.1 Provider will retain Data accessible via the API for a minimum of 12 months from the date of original creation.  
6.2 Upon termination or expiration of the Term, Provider will retain Client’s Data for 30 days to allow for retrieval, after which Data will be deleted.

7. SECURITY OBLIGATIONS

7.2 Client shall promptly notify Provider of any actual or suspected unauthorized access to the API or Data.

8. SUSPENSION RIGHTS  
8.1 Provider may suspend Client’s access to the API and Data immediately upon written notice if (a) Client breaches the Access Scope, Rate Limits, or Permitted Use provisions; (b) Fees or Overage Charges are more than 30 days overdue; or (c) Provider reasonably believes suspension is necessary to protect the security or integrity of the API or Data.  
8.2 Provider will use reasonable efforts to provide advance notice and to restore access promptly after resolution of the cause for suspension.

9. LIABILITY CAP  
9.1 EXCEPT FOR CLIENT’S PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS, EACH PARTY’S AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID BY CLIENT TO PROVIDER IN THE 12 MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.  
9.2 NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT.

10. TERM  
10.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for an initial term of 12 months (“Initial Term”).  
10.2 Thereafter, the Agreement will automatically renew for successive 12-month periods unless either party provides at least 60 days’ written notice of non-renewal prior to the end of the then-current term.

11. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
11.1 Sections 3 (Permitted Use), 6 (Data Retention Period), 7 (Security Obligations), 9 (Liability Cap), and 11 (Survival / Miscellaneous) shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.  
11.2 Client’s right to access the API and Data terminates upon expiration or termination of the Term, except as provided in Section 6.2.  
11.3 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses set forth in the signature block.  
11.4 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements.  
11.5 This Agreement may only be amended in writing signed by both parties.  
11.6 If any provision is held invalid, the remainder will remain in effect.

EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

This Employment Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of June 15, 2024 (“Effective Date”) by and between Acme Solutions, Inc., a Delaware corporation (“Company”), and Jordan Lee (“Employee”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Base Salary” means the annual salary paid to Employee as set forth in Section 3.  
1.2 “Bonus” means the discretionary annual incentive payment described in Section 4.  
1.3 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information regarding Company’s business, clients, technology, and finances as further described in Section 8.  
1.4 “Notice Period” means the period of advance written notice required for termination, as set forth in Section 7.  
1.5 “Non-Compete Period” means the period following termination during which Employee is restricted from certain competitive activities, as set forth in Section 9.  
1.6 “Severance” means the post-termination compensation described in Section 10.  
1.7 “Vacation Accrual” means the rate at which Employee accrues paid vacation, as set forth in Section 5.

2. TITLE AND DUTIES  
2.1 Employee shall serve as Senior Project Manager and shall report to the Chief Operating Officer.  
2.2 Employee’s duties shall include overseeing project teams, managing client deliverables, ensuring service level agreements (“SLAs”) of 98% on-time delivery, and other duties as assigned by Company.  
2.3 Employee shall devote full working time and attention to Company and shall not engage in other employment during the term of this Agreement.

3. BASE SALARY  
3.1 Company shall pay Employee a Base Salary of $120,000 per year, payable in accordance with Company’s standard payroll practices and subject to applicable withholdings.

4. BONUS ELIGIBILITY  
4.1 Employee shall be eligible for an annual Bonus of up to 15% of Base Salary, subject to Company’s discretion and achievement of performance targets, including maintaining a minimum SLA of 98% on assigned projects.  
4.2 Bonus, if any, shall be paid within 60 days following the end of the fiscal year.

5. VACATION ACCRUAL  
5.1 Employee shall accrue paid vacation at a rate of 1.67 days per month, up to a maximum accrual cap of 20 days per year.  
5.2 Unused vacation may be carried over to the next year, subject to a maximum accrual of 30 days.

6. TERM  
6.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue until terminated by either party in accordance with Section 7.

7. TERMINATION AND NOTICE PERIOD  
7.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing 30 days’ written notice (“Notice Period”) to the other party.  
7.2 Company may terminate Employee immediately for Cause, defined as gross misconduct, material breach of this Agreement, or willful neglect of duties.  
7.3 During the Notice Period, Employee shall continue to perform duties unless otherwise directed by Company.

8. CONFIDENTIALITY  
8.1 Employee shall not, during or after employment, disclose or use any Confidential Information except as required to perform duties for Company.  
8.2 Confidential Information does not include information that is publicly available through no fault of Employee.  
8.3 Upon termination, Employee shall return all Company property and Confidential Information.

9. NON-COMPETE  
9.1 For a period of 12 months following termination of employment (“Non-Compete Period”), Employee shall not, within the states of California, New York, or Texas, directly or indirectly engage in employment with, or provide services to, any business that directly competes with Company’s core business of enterprise software consulting.  
9.2 Employee acknowledges that this restriction is reasonable and necessary to protect Company’s legitimate business interests.

10. SEVERANCE  
10.1 If Company terminates Employee without Cause and Employee signs a standard release of claims, Company shall pay Severance equal to three (3) months of Base Salary, payable in accordance with normal payroll practices.  
10.2 No Severance shall be paid if Employee resigns or is terminated for Cause.

11. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
11.1 Sections 8 (Confidentiality), 9 (Non-Compete), and 10 (Severance) shall survive termination of this Agreement for the durations specified therein.  
11.2 Employee’s obligations regarding Confidential Information remain in effect indefinitely.  
11.3 The Notice Period is 30 days, the Non-Compete Period is 12 months, and Severance, if applicable, is three (3) months of Base Salary.  
11.4 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
11.5 Any amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.  
11.6 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

_________________________  
Jordan Lee (“Employee”)

_________________________  
Acme Solutions, Inc. (“Company”)
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Position and Reporting: Jordan Lee is employed as Senior Project Manager, reporting to the Chief Operating Officer, and is required to dedicate full working hours exclusively to Acme Solutions, Inc.

- Compensation: The annual base salary is $120,000, paid according to the company’s regular payroll schedule and subject to standard deductions.

- Bonus: Employee may receive an annual bonus of up to 15% of base salary, contingent on company discretion and meeting performance goals, including achieving at least a 98% on-time delivery rate for projects. Any awarded bonus will be paid within 60 days after the fiscal year ends.

- Vacation: Paid vacation accrues at 1.67 days per month, with a yearly maximum of 20 days. Unused vacation can be carried over, but total accrued vacation cannot exceed 30 days.

- Term and Termination: The agreement is effective from June 15, 2024, and continues until ended by either party. Either party may terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days’ written notice. The company may terminate employment immediately for gross misconduct, significant breach of agreement, or willful neglect of duties. During the notice period, the employee must continue to fulfill job responsibilities unless instructed otherwise.

- Confidentiality: The employee must not disclose or use any non-public company information during or after employment, except as necessary for job duties. This obligation does not apply to information that becomes public through no fault of the employee. All company property and confidential materials must be returned upon termination. The confidentiality obligation has no time limit.

- Non-Compete: For 12 months after employment ends, the employee is prohibited from working for or providing services to any direct competitor in enterprise software consulting within California, New York, or Texas.

- Severance: If the company terminates the employee without cause and the employee signs a standard release, severance pay equal to three months of base salary will be provided, paid according to normal payroll practices. No severance is owed if the employee resigns or is terminated for cause.

- Survival: The confidentiality, non-compete, and severance provisions remain effective after employment ends, for the periods specified.

- Governing Law: The agreement is governed by Delaware law.

- Amendments: Any changes to the agreement must be made in writing and signed by both parties.

- Entire Agreement: This document replaces all previous agreements between the parties.

This Consulting Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Consultant as identified in the signature block below.

1. DEFINITIONS

3.2 Acceptance of Deliverables shall occur when Client notifies Consultant in writing of acceptance, or within five (5) business days after delivery if no rejection notice is provided.

4. RATES AND INVOICING  
4.1 Client shall pay Consultant Fees at the rate of $200 per hour, not to exceed $16,000 in aggregate for the Term unless otherwise agreed in writing.  
4.2 Consultant shall invoice Client monthly in arrears, detailing hours worked, Services performed, and any Expenses.  
4.3 Payment is due within thirty (30) days of Client’s receipt of a correct invoice.

5. EXPENSE CAPS

5.2 Any Expenses in excess of this cap shall not be reimbursed unless expressly authorized in writing by Client.

6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OWNERSHIP  
6.1 All right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product shall vest in Client upon full payment of Fees and Expenses.

6.3 Consultant retains no rights in the Work Product and shall not use or disclose Work Product except as necessary to perform the Services.

7. NON-SOLICITATION  
7.1 During the Term and for twelve (12) months thereafter, neither party shall, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or engagement any employee or contractor of the other party who was involved in the performance of the Services, without the prior written consent of the other party.

8. TERMINATION FOR CONVENIENCE  
8.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon fourteen (14) days’ prior written notice to the other party.  
8.2 Upon termination, Consultant shall deliver to Client all completed and in-progress Deliverables, and Client shall pay Consultant for all Services performed and Expenses incurred through the effective date of termination, subject to the caps in Sections 4 and 5.

9. INDEMNITY  
9.1 Consultant shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its affiliates, officers, directors, and employees from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or relating to (a) Consultant’s breach of this Agreement, or (b) any claim that the Work Product infringes any intellectual property right of a third party.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 6 (Intellectual Property Ownership), 7 (Non-Solicitation), 9 (Indemnity), and this Section 10 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.  
10.2 The non-solicitation obligation in Section 7 shall survive for twelve (12) months following termination.  
10.3 The expense cap in Section 5 and the payment obligations in Section 4 shall apply through the effective date of termination.  
10.4 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.5 Any amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.  
10.6 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of [Governing Law State], without regard to its conflict of law principles.

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Controller” means the entity that determines the purposes and means of the Processing of Personal Data.  
1.2 “Processor” means the entity that Processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.  
1.3 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person Processed by Processor on behalf of Controller under the Agreement.  
1.4 “Processing” or “Process” means any operation or set of operations performed on Personal Data, whether or not by automated means, including collection, recording, organization, structuring, storage, adaptation, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure, dissemination, alignment, combination, restriction, erasure, or destruction.  
1.5 “Sub-Processor” means any third party engaged by Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
1.6 “Data Protection Laws” means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the EU General Data Protection Regulation 2016/679 (“GDPR”).  
1.7 “Data Breach” means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.

2. ROLES OF THE PARTIES  
2.1 Controller appoints Processor to Process Personal Data on its behalf as set out in this Data Processing Addendum (“DPA”).  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, unless required to do so by law.

3. SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
3.1 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are described in Annex 1 to this DPA.  
3.2 Processor shall not Process Personal Data for any purpose other than as set forth in the Agreement and this DPA.

4. SUB-PROCESSORS  
4.1 Controller authorizes Processor to engage Sub-Processors for the Processing of Personal Data, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and notifies Controller at least 15 days in advance of any intended changes;  
(b) Controller may object to a new Sub-Processor within 10 days of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors equivalent to those set out in this DPA;  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for Sub-Processors’ acts and omissions.

5. SECURITY MEASURES  
5.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, at a minimum:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) measures to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) procedures for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of security measures.  
5.2 Processor shall ensure that persons authorized to Process Personal Data are bound by confidentiality obligations.

6. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION  
6.1 Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within 36 hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data.  
6.2 Such notification shall include, to the extent possible, a description of the nature of the Data Breach, likely consequences, and measures taken or proposed to address the Data Breach.

7. AUDIT RIGHTS  
7.1 Controller may, up to once per calendar year and upon at least 30 days’ written notice, audit Processor’s compliance with this DPA, including by inspecting facilities and reviewing documentation.  
7.2 Processor shall cooperate with such audits and provide reasonable assistance.  
7.3 Audits shall be conducted during normal business hours and shall not unreasonably interfere with Processor’s business.

8. DELETION AND RETURN OF DATA  
8.1 Upon termination or expiry of the Agreement, Processor shall, at Controller’s choice, delete or return all Personal Data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless retention is required by law.  
8.2 Processor shall certify in writing to Controller that such deletion has been completed within 30 days of termination or expiry.

9. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS  
9.1 Processor shall not transfer Personal Data outside the European Economic Area (“EEA”) or the United Kingdom unless it has implemented appropriate safeguards in accordance with Data Protection Laws, such as Standard Contractual Clauses or an adequacy decision.  
9.2 Processor shall provide Controller with details of such safeguards upon request.

10. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 5 (Security Measures), 6 (Data Breach Notification), 7 (Audit Rights), 8 (Deletion and Return of Data), and 10 (Survival and Miscellaneous) shall survive termination or expiry of this DPA for as long as Processor retains Personal Data.  
10.2 Any limitation of liability in the Agreement shall apply to this DPA, except in the event of Processor’s gross negligence or willful misconduct.  
10.3 This DPA is governed by the law specified in the Agreement.  
10.4 If any provision of this DPA is held invalid, the remainder shall remain in effect.  
10.5 Notices under this DPA shall be in writing and delivered to the contacts specified in the Agreement.

ANNEX 1 – DETAILS OF PROCESSING  
A. Subject Matter: Processing of Personal Data as necessary to provide the services under the Agreement.  
B. Duration: For the term of the Agreement and any post-termination period during which Processor retains Personal Data in accordance with Section 8.  
C. Nature and Purpose: Storage, retrieval, transmission, and deletion of Personal Data for the purpose of providing the services.  
D. Types of Personal Data: Names, contact details, identification numbers, and any other Personal Data submitted by Controller.  
E. Categories of Data Subjects: Controller’s employees, customers, suppliers, and other individuals whose Personal Data is Processed in connection with the services.
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- The Processor is engaged by the Controller to handle Personal Data solely as instructed and in accordance with the Data Processing Addendum (DPA) and applicable law.
- Processing activities are limited to those described in Annex 1, which include storage, retrieval, transmission, and deletion of Personal Data for service provision, covering data such as names, contact details, identification numbers, and other information provided by the Controller, relating to employees, customers, suppliers, and other relevant individuals.
- The duration of Processing is for the length of the Agreement and any period thereafter during which the Processor retains Personal Data as required under Section 8.
- The Processor may use Sub-Processors, provided that:
  - An up-to-date list is maintained and the Controller is notified at least 15 days before any changes.
  - The Controller has 10 days from notification to object to a new Sub-Processor.
  - Sub-Processors are bound by data protection obligations equivalent to those in the DPA.
  - The Processor remains fully responsible for Sub-Processor actions and omissions.
- The Processor must implement and maintain security measures appropriate to the risk, including at least pseudonymization, encryption, measures for confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience, and regular testing and evaluation of these measures. All personnel authorized to Process Personal Data must be under confidentiality obligations.
- In the event of a Data Breach, the Processor must inform the Controller without undue delay and no later than 36 hours after becoming aware, providing details on the breach, its likely impact, and remedial actions taken or proposed.
- The Controller is entitled to audit the Processor’s compliance with the DPA once per calendar year, with at least 30 days’ written notice. Audits may include facility inspections and document reviews, must occur during normal business hours, and should not unreasonably disrupt the Processor’s operations. The Processor must cooperate and provide reasonable assistance.
- Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, the Processor must, at the Controller’s direction, either delete or return all Personal Data and erase any remaining copies unless retention is legally required. Written certification of deletion must be provided within 30 days of termination or expiry.
- Transfers of Personal Data outside the EEA or UK are prohibited unless appropriate safeguards (such as Standard Contractual Clauses or adequacy decisions) are in place, in line with Data Protection Laws. The Processor must provide details of these safeguards upon request.
- The obligations regarding security, breach notification, audit rights, and data deletion/return continue as long as the Processor holds Personal Data after the DPA ends.
- Any liability limitations in the main Agreement also apply to this DPA, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Processor.
- The DPA is governed by the law specified in the main Agreement. If any part of the DPA is found invalid, the rest remains effective. All notices must be in writing and sent to the contacts listed in the Agreement.

2. Section 6.1 is hereby amended to replace "36 hours" with "24 hours". The revised clause shall read: "Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within 24 hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data."

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Supplier" means Acme Software Solutions, Inc., a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Wilmington, DE 19801.  
1.2 "Reseller" means the entity identified in the signature block below.  
1.3 "Products" means the software products listed in Exhibit A, as may be updated by Supplier from time to time.  
1.4 "Territory" means the United States of America and Canada.  
1.5 "End User" means any third party customer located in the Territory that acquires Products for its own internal use and not for resale.  
1.6 "Trademarks" means the Supplier’s trademarks, service marks, and logos associated with the Products.  
1.7 "Agreement" means this Reseller Agreement, including all exhibits and amendments.

2. APPOINTMENT AND TERRITORY  
2.1 Supplier hereby appoints Reseller as a non-exclusive reseller of the Products within the Territory.  
2.2 Reseller shall not market, sell, or distribute the Products outside the Territory without Supplier’s prior written consent.

3. DISCOUNT SCHEDULE  
3.1 Supplier shall sell Products to Reseller at a discount of 30% off Supplier’s then-current list price, as set forth in Exhibit A.  
3.2 Supplier reserves the right to update the list price upon thirty (30) days’ written notice to Reseller.

4. MINIMUM PURCHASE COMMITMENT  
4.1 Reseller agrees to purchase at least $50,000 USD in aggregate list price value of Products per calendar year (“Minimum Purchase Commitment”).  
4.2 If Reseller fails to meet the Minimum Purchase Commitment, Supplier may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days’ written notice.

5. PAYMENT TERMS  
5.1 All payments for Products are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date.  
5.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
5.3 All payments shall be made in U.S. dollars by wire transfer or other method specified by Supplier.

6. TRADEMARK USE  
6.1 Supplier grants Reseller a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Trademarks solely in connection with the marketing and sale of Products in the Territory during the Term.  
6.2 All use of Trademarks by Reseller shall comply with Supplier’s trademark usage guidelines, as provided and updated from time to time.  
6.3 Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Reseller shall immediately cease all use of the Trademarks.

7. TERM AND TERMINATION  
7.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of one (1) year (“Initial Term”). Thereafter, it shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods unless either party provides at least sixty (60) days’ written notice of non-renewal prior to the end of the then-current term.  
7.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party, provided that the breaching party fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice.  
7.3 Supplier may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if Reseller becomes insolvent, files for bankruptcy, or ceases business operations.

8. POST-TERMINATION SELL-OFF PERIOD  
8.1 Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Reseller may continue to sell its remaining inventory of Products for a period of ninety (90) days (“Sell-Off Period”), provided that all such sales comply with the terms of this Agreement.  
8.2 After the Sell-Off Period, Reseller shall cease all sales and marketing of the Products.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
9.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR BREACH OF SECTION 6 (TRADEMARK USE), IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY’S AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNTS PAID BY RESELLER TO SUPPLIER IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.  
9.2 IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS OR REVENUE, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 5 (Payment Terms), 6.3 (Trademark Use), 8 (Post-Termination Sell-Off Period), 9 (Limitation of Liability), and 10 (Survival / Miscellaneous) shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.  
10.2 All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by certified mail or email to the addresses set forth in the signature block.  
10.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
10.5 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law principles.  
10.6 Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved in the state or federal courts located in Wilmington, Delaware.  
10.7 The obligations regarding payment, use of Trademarks, limitation of liability, and post-termination sell-off shall remain in effect for the durations specified in Sections 5, 6, 8, and 9, respectively.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

[Signature Blocks for Supplier and Reseller]
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Parties: The agreement is between Acme Software Solutions, Inc. (“Supplier”) and the Reseller named in the signature block.
- Products: The software products covered are those listed in Exhibit A, as updated by Supplier.
- Territory: Reseller is authorized to sell Products only within the United States and Canada.
- Appointment: Reseller is granted a non-exclusive right to market and distribute the Products in the Territory. Sales outside this area require Supplier’s prior written approval.
- Pricing and Discounts: Products are sold to Reseller at a 30% discount off Supplier’s current list price. Supplier may change the list price with 30 days’ written notice.
- Minimum Purchase: Reseller must buy at least $50,000 USD (based on list price) of Products each calendar year. If this threshold is not met, Supplier can terminate the agreement with 30 days’ written notice.
- Payment Terms: Payment is due within 30 days of invoice. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower. Payments must be made in U.S. dollars via wire transfer or another method specified by Supplier.
- Trademark Usage: Reseller receives a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use Supplier’s trademarks solely for marketing and selling Products in the Territory during the agreement’s term. All trademark use must follow Supplier’s guidelines. Upon termination or expiration, Reseller must immediately stop using the trademarks.
- Term and Renewal: The agreement starts on the Effective Date and lasts for one year. It automatically renews for additional one-year periods unless either party gives at least 60 days’ written notice before the current term ends.
- Termination: Either party can terminate for material breach if the breach is not remedied within 30 days of written notice. Supplier may terminate immediately if Reseller becomes insolvent, files for bankruptcy, or ceases operations.
- Post-Termination Sales: After termination or expiration, Reseller may sell remaining inventory for up to 90 days, provided all sales comply with the agreement. After this period, all sales and marketing must stop.
- Liability Limits: Except for gross negligence, willful misconduct, or trademark misuse, each party’s total liability is capped at the amount paid by Reseller to Supplier in the 12 months before the claim. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, including lost profits or revenue.
- Survival: Provisions regarding payment, trademark use after termination, post-termination sales, and liability limitations remain effective as specified.
- Notices: All notices must be in writing and sent by certified mail or email to the addresses in the signature block.
- Governing Law and Venue: Delaware law governs the agreement, and disputes are to be resolved in Wilmington, Delaware courts.
- Entire Agreement and Amendments: This document is the complete agreement and overrides prior agreements. Changes or waivers must be in writing and signed by both parties.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO RESELLER AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made to the Reseller Agreement (the "Agreement") by and between Acme Software Solutions, Inc. ("Supplier") and Reseller, effective as of the date of last signature below.

1. Section 3.1 (Discount Schedule) is hereby amended by replacing the phrase "a discount of 30% off Supplier’s then-current list price" with "a discount of 35% off Supplier’s then-current list price."

2. Section 8.1 (Post-Termination Sell-Off Period) is hereby amended by replacing the phrase "for a period of ninety (90) days ('Sell-Off Period')" with "for a period of sixty (60) days ('Sell-Off Period')".

Except as expressly amended herein, all other terms and conditions of the Agreement remain in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Amendment as of the dates set forth below.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Landlord" means Acme Properties LLC, with a principal address at 100 Main Street, Springfield, State of Example.  
1.2 "Tenant" means Beta Retail, Inc., with a principal address at 200 Commerce Avenue, Springfield, State of Example.  
1.3 "Premises" means the commercial space located at 123 Market Street, Suite 400, Springfield, State of Example, consisting of approximately 2,500 rentable square feet.  
1.4 "Base Rent" means the initial annual rent of $60,000, payable in equal monthly installments of $5,000.  
1.5 "Escalation Percentage" means an annual increase of 3% applied to the Base Rent on each anniversary of the Commencement Date.  
1.6 "Security Deposit" means the sum of $10,000 to be held by Landlord as security for Tenant’s obligations.  
1.7 "Commencement Date" means July 1, 2024.  
1.8 "Term" means the initial lease period of five (5) years commencing on the Commencement Date.  
1.9 "Renewal Option" means Tenant’s right to renew the Term for one (1) additional period of three (3) years, subject to Section 5.  
1.10 "Default Cure Period" means the period of ten (10) business days after written notice of default is given by Landlord to Tenant.  
1.11 "Assignment" means any transfer, sublease, or assignment of Tenant’s interest in this Lease, as governed by Section 8.

2. PREMISES  
2.1 Landlord hereby leases to Tenant, and Tenant hereby leases from Landlord, the Premises as defined above, together with the right to use common areas as designated by Landlord.

3. BASE RENT AND ESCALATION  
3.1 Tenant shall pay to Landlord the Base Rent in advance on the first day of each calendar month.  
3.2 On each anniversary of the Commencement Date, the Base Rent shall increase by the Escalation Percentage.  
3.3 All rent payments shall be made to Landlord at the address specified above or as otherwise directed in writing.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT  
4.1 Upon execution of this Lease, Tenant shall deposit with Landlord the Security Deposit.  
4.2 The Security Deposit shall be held by Landlord as security for the full and faithful performance by Tenant of all terms of this Lease.  
4.3 Landlord may apply all or part of the Security Deposit to cure any default by Tenant, and Tenant shall promptly restore the Security Deposit to its original amount upon demand.

5. TERM AND RENEWAL OPTION  
5.1 The Term of this Lease shall be five (5) years, commencing on the Commencement Date and expiring on June 30, 2029.  
5.2 Tenant may exercise the Renewal Option by providing written notice to Landlord at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the initial Term.  
5.3 The Renewal Option shall be on the same terms and conditions as this Lease, except that Base Rent during the renewal period shall be the greater of (a) the Base Rent in effect at the end of the initial Term increased by the Escalation Percentage, or (b) the then-prevailing market rent for comparable premises, as determined by Landlord in good faith.

6. MAINTENANCE OBLIGATIONS  
6.1 Landlord shall maintain the structural components, roof, and exterior of the Premises in good repair, with a service level agreement (SLA) of 95% completion of repairs within ten (10) business days of notice.  
6.2 Tenant shall, at its sole expense, maintain the interior of the Premises, including all fixtures, equipment, and non-structural elements, in a clean and safe condition.

7. DEFAULT AND REMEDIES  
7.1 If Tenant fails to pay any rent or other sum due, or otherwise defaults in the performance of any obligation under this Lease, Landlord shall provide written notice to Tenant specifying the nature of the default.  
7.2 Tenant shall have the Default Cure Period to cure such default.  
7.3 If Tenant fails to cure the default within the Default Cure Period, Landlord may, at its option, terminate this Lease and/or pursue any remedies available at law or in equity.

8. ASSIGNMENT  
8.1 Tenant shall not assign, sublease, or otherwise transfer its interest in this Lease or the Premises without the prior written consent of Landlord, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed.  
8.2 Any attempted Assignment without Landlord’s consent shall be void and constitute a default under this Lease.

9. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
9.1 All obligations of Tenant for payment of rent, restoration of the Security Deposit, and indemnification shall survive the expiration or earlier termination of this Lease.  
9.2 The Term, including any Renewal Option exercised by Tenant, shall not exceed eight (8) years in total.  
9.3 Notices under this Lease shall be in writing and delivered to the addresses set forth in Section 1 by certified mail or recognized overnight courier.  
9.4 This Lease constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
9.5 No amendment or modification of this Lease shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
9.6 If any provision of this Lease is held invalid, the remainder shall not be affected.  
9.7 This Lease shall be governed by the laws of the State of Example.
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Parties: The lease is between Acme Properties LLC (Landlord) and Beta Retail, Inc. (Tenant).
- Premises: The Tenant is leasing approximately 2,500 rentable square feet at 123 Market Street, Suite 400, Springfield, State of Example, including access to common areas as designated by the Landlord.
- Lease Term: The initial lease period is five years, starting July 1, 2024, and ending June 30, 2029.
- Renewal Option: The Tenant may extend the lease for one additional three-year term by giving written notice to the Landlord at least 180 days before the initial term ends. The renewal will be on the same terms, except the rent will be the higher of (a) the escalated Base Rent at the end of the initial term or (b) the current market rent for similar spaces, as determined by the Landlord in good faith. The total lease duration, including renewal, cannot exceed eight years.
- Base Rent: The starting annual rent is $60,000, payable in monthly installments of $5,000, due in advance on the first day of each month.
- Rent Escalation: Each year on the anniversary of the lease start date, the Base Rent increases by 3%.
- Security Deposit: The Tenant must provide a $10,000 security deposit upon signing. The Landlord holds this as security for the Tenant’s obligations and may use it to cover any defaults. If any portion is used, the Tenant must replenish the deposit to its original amount upon request.
- Maintenance: The Landlord is responsible for maintaining the building’s structure, roof, and exterior, with a commitment to complete 95% of repairs within ten business days of receiving notice. The Tenant is responsible for maintaining the interior, including fixtures, equipment, and non-structural elements, at its own cost.
- Default and Cure: If the Tenant defaults (including non-payment of rent), the Landlord must provide written notice specifying the issue. The Tenant then has ten business days to remedy the default. If not cured within this period, the Landlord may terminate the lease and/or seek other legal remedies.
- Assignment/Subletting: The Tenant cannot assign, sublease, or transfer its lease interest without the Landlord’s prior written consent, which cannot be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Any unauthorized transfer is void and considered a default.
- Notices: All formal communications must be in writing and delivered by certified mail or recognized overnight courier to the addresses specified for each party.
- Survival: Tenant’s obligations regarding rent, security deposit restoration, and indemnification continue beyond the lease’s expiration or termination.
- Governing Law: The lease is governed by the laws of the State of Example.
- Entire Agreement and Amendments: This document represents the full agreement between the parties and overrides any previous agreements. Changes must be in writing and signed by both parties.
- Severability: If any part of the lease is found invalid, the rest remains effective.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made as of the date set forth below by and between Acme Properties LLC ("Landlord") and Beta Retail, Inc. ("Tenant").

WHEREAS, the parties entered into that certain Commercial Lease Agreement dated prior to this Amendment (the "Lease");

NOW, THEREFORE, for good and valuable consideration, the parties agree to amend the Lease as follows:

1. Section 1.4 ("Base Rent") is hereby amended to change the initial annual rent from $60,000 to $66,000, payable in equal monthly installments of $5,500.

2. Section 6.1 is hereby amended to change the service level agreement (SLA) for completion of repairs from 95% completion within ten (10) business days of notice to 98% completion within seven (7) business days of notice.

All other terms and conditions of the Lease remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Amendment as of the date written below.
