JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Agreement” means this Joint Venture Agreement, including all schedules and amendments.  
1.2 “JV” means the joint venture formed pursuant to this Agreement.  
1.3 “Parties” means Alpha Corp., a Delaware corporation with principal offices at 123 Main Street, Wilmington, DE (“Alpha”), and Beta LLC, a New York limited liability company with principal offices at 456 Park Avenue, New York, NY (“Beta”).  
1.4 “Business” means the development, marketing, and sale of electric vehicle charging stations in the United States.  
1.5 “Capital Contributions” means the cash, property, or services contributed by each Party to the JV as set forth in Section 3.  
1.6 “Profits and Losses” means the net income or loss of the JV as determined in accordance with U.S. GAAP.  
1.7 “Board” means the governing body of the JV as described in Section 5.  
1.8 “Deadlock” means a situation where the Board is unable to reach a decision on a Reserved Matter after two consecutive votes.  
1.9 “Reserved Matters” means those matters listed in Section 5.4 requiring a Supermajority Vote.  
1.10 “Supermajority Vote” means approval by at least 75% of the Board members.

2. PURPOSE AND SCOPE  
2.1 The purpose of the JV is to engage in the Business as defined above.  
2.2 The JV shall operate exclusively within the United States and shall not engage in any business outside this scope without a Supermajority Vote.

3. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTIONS  
3.1 Alpha shall contribute $1,000,000 in cash and assign all rights to its proprietary charging technology to the JV within 30 days of the Effective Date.  
3.2 Beta shall contribute $1,000,000 in cash and provide access to its existing distribution network within 30 days of the Effective Date.  
3.3 No additional Capital Contributions shall be required unless approved by a Supermajority Vote of the Board.

4. PROFITS AND LOSSES  
4.1 Profits and Losses of the JV shall be allocated 50% to Alpha and 50% to Beta.  
4.2 Distributions of available cash shall be made quarterly, within 30 days after the end of each fiscal quarter, in accordance with the allocation of Profits and Losses.

5. GOVERNANCE AND VOTING  
5.1 The Board shall consist of four (4) members, two (2) appointed by Alpha and two (2) appointed by Beta.  
5.2 Each Board member shall have one (1) vote.  
5.3 Ordinary matters shall be decided by a simple majority of the Board.  
5.4 The following Reserved Matters require a Supermajority Vote:  
  (a) Amendment of the JV’s business plan;  
  (b) Approval of annual budgets;  
  (c) Admission of new members;  
  (d) Sale or transfer of all or substantially all JV assets;  
  (e) Dissolution of the JV.  
5.5 Board meetings shall be held at least quarterly, with at least ten (10) business days’ written notice to all members.

6. DEADLOCK RESOLUTION  
6.1 In the event of a Deadlock, the matter shall be referred to the Chief Executive Officers of Alpha and Beta for resolution within fifteen (15) business days.  
6.2 If the Deadlock remains unresolved, either Party may initiate binding arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association, with the seat of arbitration in New York, NY.  
6.3 The arbitrator’s decision shall be final and binding on the Parties.

7. TRANSFER RESTRICTIONS  
7.1 No Party may transfer, assign, or encumber its interest in the JV without the prior written consent of the other Party, except to an Affiliate with at least 30 days’ prior written notice.  
7.2 Any purported transfer in violation of this Section shall be null and void.

8. TERM  
8.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for five (5) years, unless earlier dissolved in accordance with Section 9.  
8.2 The Agreement may be renewed for additional three (3) year terms by mutual written agreement of the Parties at least ninety (90) days prior to expiration.

9. DISSOLUTION  
9.1 The JV may be dissolved by:  
  (a) Supermajority Vote of the Board;  
  (b) Expiration of the term without renewal;  
  (c) Sale of all or substantially all JV assets;  
  (d) Final, non-appealable order of a court of competent jurisdiction.  
9.2 Upon dissolution, the assets of the JV shall be liquidated and distributed in the following order:  
  (a) Payment of all debts and liabilities;  
  (b) Return of Capital Contributions to the Parties pro rata;  
  (c) Distribution of any remaining assets in accordance with Section 4.1.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 6 (Deadlock Resolution), 7 (Transfer Restrictions), 9 (Dissolution), and this Section 10 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.  
10.2 All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by certified mail or email with confirmation of receipt, to the addresses set forth above, or as otherwise notified in writing.  
10.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the JV and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.4 No amendment shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.  
10.5 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remainder shall remain in full force and effect.  
10.6 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.  
10.7 The obligations regarding Capital Contributions, Profits and Losses, and Deadlock Resolution shall remain in effect for the duration of the JV and any applicable wind-down period.
SUMMARY

SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS

- Parties: The joint venture (“JV”) is formed between Alpha Corp. (Delaware corporation) and Beta LLC (New York limited liability company).

- Purpose and Scope: The JV is established to develop, market, and sell electric vehicle charging stations within the United States. Any expansion beyond this territory requires approval by at least 75% of the Board.

- Capital Contributions: Both Alpha and Beta are required to contribute $1,000,000 in cash within 30 days of the agreement’s effective date. Alpha must also transfer its proprietary charging technology rights, while Beta must provide access to its distribution network within the same timeframe. Additional contributions are only required if approved by a 75% Board majority.

- Profit and Loss Sharing: Net profits and losses are split equally (50/50) between Alpha and Beta. Cash distributions are made quarterly, within 30 days after each fiscal quarter ends, in line with this allocation.

- Governance: The Board consists of four members, with each party appointing two. Each member has one vote. Routine decisions are made by simple majority. Certain key decisions (business plan changes, annual budgets, new member admissions, sale of major assets, or dissolution) require at least 75% Board approval. Board meetings must occur at least quarterly, with a minimum of 10 business days’ written notice.

- Deadlock Resolution: If the Board cannot resolve a Reserved Matter after two votes, the issue is escalated to the CEOs of both parties, who have 15 business days to resolve it. If unresolved, either party may initiate binding arbitration under the American Arbitration Association in New York, NY. The arbitrator’s decision is final.

- Transfer Restrictions: No party may transfer, assign, or encumber its JV interest without the other party’s written consent, except transfers to an Affiliate with at least 30 days’ prior written notice. Unauthorized transfers are void.

- Term and Renewal: The agreement is effective for five years from the start date, unless dissolved earlier. It can be renewed for additional three-year periods if both parties agree in writing at least 90 days before expiration.

- Dissolution: The JV may be dissolved by a 75% Board vote, expiration without renewal, sale of all or most assets, or a final court order. Upon dissolution, assets are liquidated to pay debts, then capital is returned to the parties proportionally, with any remaining assets distributed according to the 50/50 profit and loss split.

- Survival and Miscellaneous: Provisions on deadlock resolution, transfer restrictions, dissolution, and miscellaneous terms remain effective after termination. Notices must be in writing and sent by certified mail or email with confirmation. Amendments require written agreement by both parties. The agreement is governed by New York law. If any part is invalid, the rest remains enforceable. Obligations regarding contributions, profit/loss sharing, and deadlock resolution continue through the JV’s life and any wind-down period.
AMENDMENT

AMENDMENT TO JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT

This Amendment is made effective as of the date of last signature below, by and between Alpha Corp. ("Alpha") and Beta LLC ("Beta").

1. Section 3.1 is hereby amended to replace the amount of Alpha's required cash contribution from $1,000,000 to $1,500,000. The revised Section 3.1 shall read:
"Alpha shall contribute $1,500,000 in cash and assign all rights to its proprietary charging technology to the JV within 30 days of the Effective Date."

2. Section 8.1 is hereby amended to replace the initial term of the Agreement from five (5) years to seven (7) years. The revised Section 8.1 shall read:
"The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for seven (7) years, unless earlier dissolved in accordance with Section 9."

All other terms and conditions of the Agreement remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Amendment as of the dates written below.

DISTRIBUTION AND LOGISTICS AGREEMENT

This Distribution and Logistics Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the undersigned parties (“Distributor” and “Supplier,” collectively, the “Parties”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Products” means the goods supplied by Supplier to Distributor for distribution under this Agreement.  
1.2 “Territory” means the United States of America and Canada.  
1.3 “Delivery Window” means the period commencing at 8:00 AM and ending at 6:00 PM local time on a Business Day.  
1.4 “Service Level” means the percentage of deliveries completed within the Delivery Window, as set forth in Section 4.  
1.5 “Fuel Surcharge Cap” means the maximum allowable fuel surcharge of 8% of the invoiced freight charges per shipment.  
1.6 “Loss or Damage” means physical loss of or damage to Products while in Distributor’s possession or control.  
1.7 “Claims Notice Period” means the period of ten (10) Business Days from the date of delivery or scheduled delivery, whichever is earlier.  
1.8 “Term” means the period described in Section 9.  
1.9 “Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or federal holiday in the United States.

2. TERRITORY AND EXCLUSIVITY  
2.1 Supplier hereby appoints Distributor as its exclusive distributor and logistics provider for the Products within the Territory.  
2.2 Supplier shall not appoint any other distributor or logistics provider for the Products in the Territory during the Term.

3. PRICING AND FUEL SURCHARGE  
3.1 Distributor shall provide logistics and distribution services at the rates set forth in Exhibit A.  
3.2 Any fuel surcharge applied by Distributor shall not exceed the Fuel Surcharge Cap.  
3.3 Pricing may be reviewed annually, with any adjustments subject to mutual written agreement.

4. SERVICE LEVELS AND DELIVERY WINDOWS  
4.1 Distributor shall deliver at least 97% of all shipments within the Delivery Window each calendar month.  
4.2 If Distributor fails to meet the Service Level for two (2) consecutive months, Supplier may require a corrective action plan.  
4.3 Persistent failure to meet Service Levels for four (4) months in any twelve (12) month period constitutes grounds for termination under Section 10.

5. LIABILITY FOR LOSS OR DAMAGE  
5.1 Distributor shall be liable for Loss or Damage to Products occurring while in its possession or control, up to a maximum of $100,000 per occurrence.  
5.2 Liability shall not extend to Loss or Damage caused by force majeure events as defined in Section 12.  
5.3 Distributor’s liability for consequential, indirect, or punitive damages is excluded.

6. INSURANCE  
6.1 Distributor shall maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance coverage:  
(a) Commercial General Liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence;  
(b) Cargo insurance covering Loss or Damage to Products up to $100,000 per occurrence;  
(c) Automobile Liability insurance with limits of not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence.  
6.2 Upon request, Distributor shall provide Supplier with certificates of insurance evidencing the required coverage.

7. CLAIMS NOTICE PERIOD  
7.1 Supplier shall notify Distributor in writing of any claim for Loss or Damage within the Claims Notice Period.  
7.2 Failure to provide notice within the Claims Notice Period shall release Distributor from liability for such claim.

8. INDEMNIFICATION  
8.1 Each Party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other Party from and against any third-party claims, damages, or expenses arising from its own negligence or willful misconduct in connection with this Agreement.

9. TERM  
9.1 The initial Term of this Agreement shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date.  
9.2 The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then-current Term.

10. TERMINATION  
10.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other Party, provided written notice is given and the breach is not cured within thirty (30) days.  
10.2 Either Party may terminate for convenience upon one hundred twenty (120) days’ prior written notice.  
10.3 Termination shall not affect obligations accrued prior to the effective date of termination.

11. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
11.1 Sections 5 (Liability for Loss or Damage), 6 (Insurance), 7 (Claims Notice Period), 8 (Indemnification), and this Section 11 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years.  
11.2 All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered to the addresses set forth in Exhibit B.  
11.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior agreements regarding its subject matter.  
11.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.  
11.5 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. FORCE MAJEURE  
12.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement due to events beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, war, terrorism, labor disputes, or governmental actions, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice to the other Party.

MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the parties identified below (“Party” or “Parties”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Confidential Information” means any non-public, proprietary, or confidential data, information, or materials disclosed by either Party (“Disclosing Party”) to the other Party (“Receiving Party”), whether in written, oral, electronic, or other form, including but not limited to business plans, financial data, technical information, trade secrets, customer lists, and product designs.  
1.2 “Permitted Purpose” means the evaluation, negotiation, or performance of a potential or existing business relationship between the Parties.  
1.3 “Representatives” means a Party’s employees, officers, directors, agents, and professional advisors who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those in this Agreement.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature below.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENTIALITY  
2.1 The Receiving Party shall:  
(a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Permitted Purpose;  
(b) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except its Representatives;  
(c) protect the Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care;  
(d) ensure its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement.

3. EXCLUSIONS  
3.1 Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records:  
(a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement;  
(b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party;  
(c) is received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or  
(d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.  
3.2 The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, regulation, or court order, provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party at least ten (10) business days’ prior written notice (unless prohibited by law) and cooperates with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.

4. TERM AND SURVIVAL  
4.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in effect for two (2) years, unless terminated earlier by mutual written agreement of the Parties.  
4.2 The obligations of confidentiality and non-use with respect to Confidential Information shall survive for a period of three (3) years following the expiration or termination of this Agreement.

5. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION OF MATERIALS  
5.1 Upon written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly, and in any event within fifteen (15) business days, return or destroy all tangible materials containing Confidential Information, including all copies, summaries, and extracts.  
5.2 The Receiving Party may retain one (1) archival copy of Confidential Information solely for the purpose of complying with legal or regulatory requirements, subject to the confidentiality obligations herein.

6. NO LICENSE OR WARRANTY  
6.1 Nothing in this Agreement grants the Receiving Party any rights, by license or otherwise, to use the Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein.  
6.2 All Confidential Information is provided “AS IS” and without any warranty, express or implied, as to its accuracy or completeness.

7. REMEDIES  
7.1 The Parties acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, or other equitable remedies in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

8. GOVERNING LAW  
8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.  
8.2 Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in New York County, New York.

9. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
9.1 The obligations of confidentiality and non-use in Sections 2, 4, 5, and 7 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for three (3) years.  
9.2 Notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by email with confirmation of receipt, or by certified mail, return receipt requested, to the addresses set forth below or as otherwise designated in writing.  
9.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, or representations, whether oral or written.  
9.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.  
9.5 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.  
9.6 Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, except to a successor in interest in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement as of the Effective Date.

CLINICAL RESEARCH SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Clinical Research Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between Sponsor and CRO (each a “Party” and collectively the “Parties”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Sponsor” means the entity contracting for the conduct of the Study.  
1.2 “CRO” means the contract research organization providing the Services.  
1.3 “Study” means the clinical research study described in Exhibit A.  
1.4 “Services” means the clinical research services to be performed by CRO as set forth in Exhibit A.  
1.5 “Milestone Payments” means the payments due to CRO upon completion of specified milestones, as detailed in Exhibit B.  
1.6 “Study Data” means all data, results, and records generated in the performance of the Study.  
1.7 “Adverse Event” means any untoward medical occurrence in a Study subject.  
1.8 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by one Party to the other in connection with this Agreement.  
1.9 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature below.

2. SCOPE OF STUDY  
2.1 CRO shall perform the Services in accordance with the protocol attached as Exhibit A, Good Clinical Practice, and all applicable laws and regulations.  
2.2 Any changes to the Study protocol or Services must be agreed in writing by both Parties.

3. MILESTONE PAYMENTS  
3.1 Sponsor shall pay CRO the Milestone Payments as set forth in Exhibit B.  
3.2 Each Milestone Payment shall be due within thirty (30) days of Sponsor’s receipt of a valid invoice and confirmation of milestone completion.  
3.3 Total payments under this Agreement shall not exceed USD $1,000,000 without prior written amendment.

4. DATA OWNERSHIP  
4.1 All Study Data shall be the exclusive property of Sponsor.  
4.2 CRO shall promptly provide Sponsor with all Study Data and related documentation upon request and at Study completion.

5. ADVERSE EVENT REPORTING  
5.1 CRO shall report all Serious Adverse Events to Sponsor within twenty-four (24) hours of becoming aware of the event.  
5.2 All other Adverse Events shall be reported to Sponsor within five (5) business days.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY  
6.1 Each Party shall maintain the confidentiality of the other Party’s Confidential Information for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure.  
6.2 Confidential Information may only be disclosed to employees, agents, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those herein.  
6.3 The obligations of this Section 6 shall not apply to information that is (a) publicly available through no fault of the receiving Party, (b) rightfully received from a third party without restriction, or (c) independently developed without use of Confidential Information.

7. AUDIT AND INSPECTION RIGHTS  
7.1 Sponsor shall have the right, upon ten (10) business days’ written notice, to audit CRO’s facilities, records, and personnel related to the Study during normal business hours, not more than twice per calendar year.  
7.2 CRO shall cooperate with any regulatory authority inspections related to the Study and shall notify Sponsor within two (2) business days of any such inspection.

8. INDEMNITY  
8.1 CRO shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Sponsor, its affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, and employees from and against any third-party claims, damages, or expenses arising from CRO’s gross negligence or willful misconduct in the performance of the Services.  
8.2 Sponsor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless CRO, its affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, and employees from and against any third-party claims, damages, or expenses arising from the Study protocol, Study drug, or Sponsor’s gross negligence or willful misconduct.

9. TERM AND TERMINATION  
9.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue until completion of the Services, unless terminated earlier as provided herein.  
9.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days’ prior written notice to the other Party.  
9.3 Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of such breach.  
9.4 Upon termination, CRO shall promptly return all Study Data and Confidential Information to Sponsor and shall be entitled to payment for Services performed up to the effective date of termination.

10. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
10.1 Sections 4 (Data Ownership), 6 (Confidentiality), 8 (Indemnity), and 10 (Survival / Miscellaneous) shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.  
10.2 Notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by courier or email with confirmation of receipt.  
10.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements.  
10.4 No amendment shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.  
10.5 Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, except to an affiliate or successor in interest.  
10.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

CONSTRUCTION SUBCONTRACT AGREEMENT

This Construction Subcontract Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Contractor and the Subcontractor as identified in the signature block below.

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Contractor” means the party identified as Contractor in the signature block.  
1.2 “Subcontractor” means the party identified as Subcontractor in the signature block.  
1.3 “Project” means the construction project located at the address specified in Exhibit A.  
1.4 “Scope of Work” means the labor, materials, equipment, and services to be provided by Subcontractor as described in Exhibit B.  
1.5 “Contract Sum” means the total amount payable to Subcontractor for satisfactory completion of the Scope of Work, as set forth in Section 3.  
1.6 “Retainage” means the percentage of each payment withheld by Contractor as security for Subcontractor’s performance, as set forth in Section 4.  
1.7 “Change Order” means a written modification to the Scope of Work, Contract Sum, or schedule, executed in accordance with Section 6.  
1.8 “Liquidated Damages” means the daily amount payable by Subcontractor for failure to achieve Substantial Completion by the agreed date, as set forth in Section 7.  
1.9 “Warranty Period” means the period during which Subcontractor warrants its work, as set forth in Section 8.  
1.10 “Insurance Limits” means the minimum insurance coverage required of Subcontractor, as set forth in Section 9.

2. SCOPE OF WORK  
2.1 Subcontractor shall furnish all labor, materials, equipment, and services necessary to complete the Scope of Work in accordance with the Project plans, specifications, and schedule.  
2.2 Subcontractor shall comply with all applicable laws, codes, and regulations.

3. CONTRACT SUM  
3.1 The Contract Sum for the Scope of Work is Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000.00), subject to adjustments by Change Order.  
3.2 The Contract Sum includes all applicable taxes, overhead, and profit.

4. RETAINAGE AND PAYMENT SCHEDULE  
4.1 Contractor shall pay Subcontractor monthly progress payments equal to ninety percent (90%) of the value of work satisfactorily performed, as determined by Contractor.  
4.2 Ten percent (10%) of each payment shall be withheld as Retainage.  
4.3 Retainage shall be released within thirty (30) days after final acceptance of the Scope of Work and receipt of all closeout documents.  
4.4 Subcontractor shall submit payment applications by the 25th day of each month. Contractor shall pay approved amounts within twenty (20) days of receipt.

5. TIME OF PERFORMANCE  
5.1 Subcontractor shall commence work within five (5) days of written notice to proceed and shall achieve Substantial Completion of the Scope of Work by October 31, 2024.  
5.2 Time is of the essence.

6. CHANGE ORDER PROCESS  
6.1 No changes to the Scope of Work, Contract Sum, or schedule shall be made except by written Change Order signed by both parties.  
6.2 Subcontractor shall submit any request for a Change Order within five (5) days of becoming aware of the circumstances giving rise to the change.  
6.3 Contractor shall respond to any Change Order request within seven (7) days.

7. LIQUIDATED DAMAGES  
7.1 If Subcontractor fails to achieve Substantial Completion by the date specified in Section 5.1, Subcontractor shall pay Contractor Liquidated Damages of Five Hundred Dollars ($500.00) per calendar day until Substantial Completion is achieved.  
7.2 Liquidated Damages are not a penalty but a reasonable estimate of actual damages.

8. WARRANTY PERIOD  
8.1 Subcontractor warrants that all work performed under this Agreement shall be free from defects in workmanship and materials for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of final acceptance by Contractor.  
8.2 Subcontractor shall promptly correct any defects discovered during the Warranty Period at its own expense.

9. INSURANCE LIMITS  
9.1 Subcontractor shall maintain the following minimum insurance coverage:  
(a) Commercial General Liability: $1,000,000 per occurrence / $2,000,000 aggregate  
(b) Workers’ Compensation: Statutory limits  
(c) Automobile Liability: $1,000,000 combined single limit  
9.2 Subcontractor shall provide certificates of insurance naming Contractor as additional insured prior to commencing work.

10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION  
10.1 Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall first be subject to good faith negotiation between the parties.  
10.2 If not resolved within fifteen (15) days, the dispute shall be submitted to non-binding mediation in the county where the Project is located.  
10.3 If mediation fails, either party may pursue legal remedies in a court of competent jurisdiction.

11. SURVIVAL / MISCELLANEOUS  
11.1 The obligations regarding Retainage (Section 4), Liquidated Damages (Section 7), Warranty Period (Section 8), and Dispute Resolution (Section 10) shall survive termination or completion of this Agreement for the durations specified.  
11.2 Notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by certified mail or email with confirmation of receipt, effective upon delivery.  
11.3 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
11.4 This Agreement may only be amended by a written instrument signed by both parties.  
11.5 If any provision is held invalid, the remainder shall remain in effect.

    - Automobile Liability: $1,000,000 combined single limit.
  - Proof of insurance, naming the Contractor as an additional insured, must be provided before work starts.

- Dispute Resolution:
  - Disputes must first be addressed through good faith negotiations.
  - If unresolved after 15 days, disputes proceed to non-binding mediation in the project’s county.
  - If mediation fails, either party may seek resolution in court.

- Survival and Miscellaneous:
  - Provisions regarding Retainage, Liquidated Damages, Warranty, and Dispute Resolution remain effective after the contract ends, for the periods specified.
  - All notices must be in writing and delivered by certified mail or email with confirmation.
  - The agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of terms and can only be changed in writing signed by both parties.
  - If any part of the agreement is found invalid, the rest remains enforceable.
